g.title: Base class - rigid disk with mass and inertia
Gear modeled as a disk with mesh properties. Inherits mass and inertia from DiskElement. Connects to another GearElement via Mesh.
Gear with time-varying mesh stiffness (TVMS). Models the stiffness variation as teeth enter and exit contact.
Returns the mesh stiffness as a function of rotational angle. Used in time-domain analyses to capture parametric excitation.
Object that connects two GearElements. Defines the stiffness and damping of the tooth contact. Calculates mesh forces (tangential, radial, axial).
GearElements and Mesh are used to create multi-rotor systems coupled by gears (gearboxes).
Number of gear teeth. Defines the transmission ratio with the coupled gear.
Pitch diameter [m] Circle where teeth mesh. pitch_diameter = n_teeth / diametral_pitch
Base diameter [m] base_diameter = pitch_diameter × cos(pr_angle) Defines the involute tooth profile
pr_angle: pressure angle [rad] (typically 20° = 0.349 rad) helix_angle: helix angle [rad] (0 for spur gears)
First gear (driving)
Second gear (driven)
Mesh stiffness [N/m] Can be constant or variable (TVMS)
Mesh damping [N·s/m]
